signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: Middle East escalation drives inflation surge, pushing Fed rate cuts to late 2026 — Reuters poll. This development supports the USD, while Japan's manufacturing surge is overshadowed by services slowdown. USD/JPY at 159.48, nearing the 160.00 level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings; intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160. The BoJ's hawkish stance is challenged by the Fed's hold, with a 300bp carry differential favoring USD. Risk-off flow historically unwinds JPY shorts — 157.00 is the first technical level on a sustained risk-off move.
evidence: Fed rate cuts pushed to late 2026

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + Retail
signals.text: AUD/USD COT LF net long +41,675 (bullish spec positioning) vs retail 89% short (extreme retail short). The positioning divergence suggests institutional investors are bullish, while retail is extremely bearish, increasing the risk of a contrarian move. HV30 at 8.2%, ATM IV at 9.4% — options pricing a 1.2 vol-pt premium over realized. 25d RR at −0.14: mild put skew despite the bullish price trend — market hedging rather than chasing. A LF delta turning positive or AM reducing would invalidate the caution read.

signals.title: EUR/USD — CB + Technical
signals.text: ECB 2% vs Fed 3.75% — 175bp EUR discount structurally pressures the pair. EUR/USD at 1.1708, mid-range; 1.1720 is the next resistance on a continuation. Positioning neutral, with EUR LF net short −72; AM constructive. The recent Middle East escalation has driven EUR/USD volatility, but the pair remains within its historical range.

signals.title: GBP/USD — CB + Technical
signals.text: BoE 3.75% vs Fed 3.75% — neutral rate differential. GBP/USD at 1.3497, mid-range; 1.3520 is the next resistance on a continuation. Positioning bullish, with GBP LF net long +28,426; AM constructive. The recent GBP/USD price action is driven by the USD's broad-based movement.

signals.title: NZD/USD — Positioning + COT
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short −17,090 (specs short) vs RBNZ OIS hike 100% priced. Specs are fading a hawkish CB. If RBNZ delivers or signals hike, short squeeze risk is elevated. NZD/USD at 0.5901, near recent lows; 0.5870 is the next support level.
